title: What did Maya see
wikitext:
[]: What did Maya see?             Quote Post by TheWaffleCult » Tue Aug 04, 2009 3:58 am When GNR first materializes, her hand passes through Maya's body. Maya's reaction is, to say the least, horrific. Generally it'd be pretty easy to write off as... well, a giant anything passing through someone would freak them the hell out. But Ibuki was present for every single Angel attack in the series - something you'd expect to harden someone's resolve as far as paranormal things go. What, if anything, happened to Maya when the GNR went through her? Her reaction seems too hysterical for it to be nothing.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 4:01 am Absolute Terror. ...I'm sorry. I couldn't resist. I don't actually have anything to contribute. But that might be a legitimate starting point regardless.

[]:             Quote Post by TheWaffleCult » Tue Aug 04, 2009 4:03 am You just field ed a good question. Zing.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 4:29 am She felt divine touch and didn't particularly like it. I think it's quite as simple as that. How would YOU feel if you were literally touched creator God/Mother like that? I don't think there's anything more in it than that. if one wants I guess you could see it as unintentional(?) assault on her AT Field that should freak her out - especially since it ain't Ritsuko

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Tue Aug 04, 2009 4:40 am She became aware that she is a character in a TV show, and broke the fourth wall by looking at the camera. Thats what Lilith does, fucks with your head.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 5:19 am Maya got fingered in a bad place and expressed her disapproval.

[]:             Quote Post by Logan Payne » Tue Aug 04, 2009 5:38 am I've wondered about that. There's a possibility she got to experience a part of Rei's... consciousness? Far more likely, though, she came to realize what was about to happen: BAD THINGS. But, considering the furious head shaking, I suspect it was: Absolutely unspeakable and unimaginable bad things. Just take Maya's word for it.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:00 am In all honesty I don't think we need any better explanation than: "Oh my God, that thing touched my insides and I don't know if it did anything to me!"

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:02 am I have always thought of being freaked out by a divine touch or something like that, but I just got a new (possible or not?) idea: Maybe she learned of Ritsuko's death somehow through GNR?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:03 am She saw Ritsuko with another woman through the touch of GNR?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:04 am Oz wrote: I have always thought of being freaked out by a divine touch or something like that, but I just got a new (possible or not?) idea: Maybe she learned of Ritsuko's death somehow through GNR?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:38 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Maya got fingered in a bad place and expressed her disapproval.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:52 am Xard wrote: we shall either go mad from the revelation or flee from the light into the peace and safety of a new dark age.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:55 am The quote you speak of is very telling. I interpret it as saying not that it is the inherent nature of knowledge to split the mind, but as the inherent nature of the human mind to be unable to accept not being split by it. Or as Touji might put it... People have a hard time acceptin' that humans ain't as special as we think.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 6:57 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Xard wrote: we shall either go mad from the revelation or flee from the light into the peace and safety of a new dark age.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:01 am Monk Ed wrote: The quote you speak of is very telling. I interpret it as saying not that it is the inherent nature of knowledge to split the mind, but as the inherent nature of the human mind to be unable to accept not being split by it.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:02 am ath wrote: Monk Ed wrote: The quote you speak of is very telling. I interpret it as saying not that it is the inherent nature of knowledge to split the mind, but as the inherent nature of the human mind to be unable to accept not being split by it.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:14 am Xard wrote: ath wrote: Fuyutsuki, in episode 21, wrote: Maybe it's more blissful not to know.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:15 am Xard wrote: you hadn't heard that before? That's surprsing, being one of his more famous ones and one that sums up his writings scaringly sufficiently

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Aug 04, 2009 7:21 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Xard wrote: you hadn't heard that before? That's surprsing, being one of his more famous ones and one that sums up his writings scaringly sufficiently

Maya's reaction is, to say the least, horrific. Generally it'd be pretty easy to write off as... well, a giant anything passing through someone would freak them the hell out. But Ibuki was present for every single Angel attack in the series - something you'd expect to harden someone's resolve as far as paranormal things go. What, if anything, happened to Maya when the GNR went through her? Her reaction seems too hysterical for it to be nothing.
